title: LOTTO is on a roll, play now to win
description: BUSINESS NEWS - ITHUBA, the proud operator...
bodyText:
Sponsored Content

BUSINESS NEWS - ITHUBA, the proud operator of the National Lottery, is thrilled to announce that LOTTO has rolled to yet another jaw-dropping jackpot, which is guaranteed at R98m this Saturday, 26 October.


> This incredible jackpot has set the country abuzz with Mzansi's biggest stars, including Moshe Ndiki, Celeste Ntuli, Clement Maosa and other household names set to hit the retail stores across all nine provinces to purchase their tickets, as they, too, hope to realise their biggest dreams.


As LOTTO participants head to their local retailers to purchase their tickets for tomorrow’s draw, they should watch out for their favourite personalities who will visit various stores in Gqeberha, Polokwane, Rustenburg, Bloemfontein, Johannesburg North and South, Cape Town, Nelspruit, Durban and Kimberly.

The second-largest jackpot in the history of LOTTO was valued at R100m and claimed by a lucky Pretoria resident in August this year, while in 2018 the largest and record-breaking LOTTO jackpot of R110m transformed the lives of 4 winners who shared the winnings.

The opportunities to win are abundant, with only R5.00, one or more lucky participants could turn their most daring dreams into reality. With an additional R2.50, participants stand a chance to win an estimated R13m jackpot on LOTTO Plus 1 and an estimated R1m jackpot on LOTTO Plus 2, which is also at an affordable cost of R2.50.

Charmaine Mabuza, ITHUBA CEO is encouraging South Africans to purchase their tickets to stand a chance to win, "We are excited to see participants flocking to their nearest retailers for a chance to transform their lives. Those who prefer other methods of play are also encouraged to go onto their banking apps, National Lottery website or mobile App or even our USSD line,” added Mabuza.

Ticket sales for this thrilling draw will close at 20:30 tonight, Saturday 26 October.

All winnings are tax free.

You must be 18 years or older to play. Play responsibly.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
